name: preview edit ops are inert: Cut / Paste / Delete cause NO side effects in a rendered preview
comment: The rendered Markdown preview is read-only. Cut, Paste, and Delete must do NOTHING there — and crucially must cause NO SIDE EFFECTS: the clipboard must be left exactly as it was (NOT cleared — NSText.cut/delete on an empty target clears the pasteboard, the same class of bug that made empty-selection Copy destroy the clipboard) and the document text must be byte-for-byte unchanged. A command that silently mutates state is worse than one that errors, because nothing tells the user it happened. This plan proves inertness by NEGATIVE CONTROL: seed a clipboard sentinel and capture the document's visible text BEFORE, fire each op through the preview, then assert the sentinel survived and the text is unchanged. Enters via AUTO-PREVIEW (the door every Markdown doc is in by default) — NOT --no-auto-preview, NOT the View menu. Requires staged /tmp/medit-ap-noop.md and the Debug build.

steps.comment: Preview must auto-open. If it doesn't, the whole premise is wrong and we should not proceed to assert inertness against the editor.

Door check: the preview web view is present (auto-preview engaged). A hidden NSTextView still exposes its AX element, so we assert the preview IS up rather than the editor is absent — the latter is always false in the AX tree even when the editor is covered.
Poll for a rendered element rather than sleeping; render is async.
Positive control: prove the seed took, so a later 'survived' assertion means something.
Snapshot the on-disk document so we can prove Cut/Delete/Paste did not mutate it. The file is the source of truth the preview renders.
Select everything first, so Cut/Delete have a non-empty visual selection to (not) act on — the worst case for accidental mutation.
Cut must NOT clear or overwrite the clipboard in a read-only preview.
Put distinctive content on the clipboard so that IF paste wrongly inserts, the document diff below will contain it.

Byte-for-byte comparison. Exit non-zero (fails the step) if Cut, Delete, or Paste mutated the document. Also greps for the paste marker as a belt-and-suspenders check that paste did not leak into the source.
